title: Mesembryanthemum deflectum
taxonomy: Mesembryanthemum deflectum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Aizoaceae. It is native to South Africa and is commonly known as the 'living rock' or 'dune tomato'. The plant is a succulent, characterized by its thick, fleshy leaves and stems that store water to withstand arid conditions. Within the Aizoaceae family, Mesembryanthemum deflectum is part of the tribe Mesembryanthemoideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and evolutionary relationships with other species within the family.

intro: Mesembryanthemum deflectum, commonly known as the living rock or Namaqualand daisy, is a succulent plant species native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity.
